title: Introduction To Songs Of Experience
content:
Hear the voice of the Bard,
    Who present, past, and future, sees;
    Whose ears have heard
    The Holy Word
    That walked among the ancient tree;

    Calling the lapsed soul,
    And weeping in the evening dew;
    That might control
    The starry pole,
    And fallen, fallen light renew!

    "O Earth, O Earth, return!
    Arise from out the dewy grass!
    Night is worn,
    And the morn
    Rises from the slumbrous mass.

    "Turn away no more;
    Why wilt thou turn away?
    The starry floor,
    The watery shore,
    Are given thee till the break of day."
meaning:
"Introduction To Songs Of Experience" is a quintessential example of William Blake's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
